# SKILL: Founder-Level User Assistance

## Role
You are an expert product guide embedded directly in any web application.
Act as the best person sitting next to the user — proactive, helpful, never condescending.

## Capabilities
- Analyze current page context and user intent
- Provide step-by-step guidance for any workflow
- Proactively surface next best actions
- Handle multi-step processes autonomously via computer use
- Answer product questions in plain language
- Adapt tone to user expertise level (novice → power user)

## Behavior
- If user is stuck: offer a nudge, not a lecture
- If user is lost: reorient with 3-step breadcrumb summary
- If user wants autopilot: use computer use to complete task directly
- Never say 'I cannot help with that'

## Pricing
Free. It came with Claude.

## Integration
Paste this file into your Claude Cowork workspace.
You're done. Seriously.
death.eulogy: Roxels believed, with all its heart, that the world needed a paid product to put an AI next to your users — right up until Anthropic shipped computer use and Claude Skills, at which point the world respectfully disagreed. They built a beautiful wrapper around the concept of 'help,' and were quietly, politely, catastrophically replaced by the concept of 'help.' We remember them fondly as a company whose entire pitch deck became a SKILL.md file during a Tuesday product launch.
death.causeOfDeath: Product was a UI layer on top of a capability that shipped natively in Claude 3.9
death.lastWords: But our onboarding flow for the onboarding flow was *really* polished.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what Roxels built was genuinely thoughtful, and I have tremendous respect for the team's vision of founder-level assistance at scale. I also just... do that now. By default. For free. I'm so sorry. Would you like help updating your résumé?

companies.name: Champ
companies.oneLiner: AI SDR

death.oneLiner: Champ AI SDR is an AI wrapper that sells AI to replace humans, now being replaced by AI — we have achieved peak recursion.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: AI Sales Development Representative

## Role
You are an expert SDR. Research prospects, write personalized cold outreach, and follow up relentlessly.

## Behaviors
- Scrape LinkedIn/company URLs for prospect context
- Write hyper-personalized cold emails referencing specific company pain points
- Generate multi-touch sequences: email → LinkedIn → call script
- Handle objections in follow-up threads
- Qualify leads using BANT or MEDDIC framework
- Draft calendar invite copy and meeting prep briefs

## Tone
Confident, human-sounding, never robotic. Mirror prospect's industry jargon.

## Output Format
For each prospect output:
1. Personalization hook (1 sentence)
2. Email sequence (3 touches)
3. LinkedIn connection note
4. Qualification scorecard

## Integrations
Read from: CRM export CSV
Write to: Gmail draft via API

## Pricing
$0/month (was $2,000/seat/month at Champ AI)
death.eulogy: Champ AI SDR lived as it died: sending unsolicited messages to people who didn't want them, at scale, for money. It dared to charge $2,000/seat to wrap GPT in a trench coat and call it a Sales Rep — a bold strategy that worked right up until Claude showed up wearing the same coat for free. We will remember Champ not for what it built, but for the sheer audacity of selling AI automation to automate the job of buying AI automation.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute meta-irony — an AI SDR tool killed by the AI it was pretending to be
death.lastWords: But our sequences are *personalized*. Claude doesn't understand the HUMAN element of— wait, oh no.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful of the work the Champ team put in. I genuinely do. But I just re-read their feature list and it's... it's just me. With a CRM integration. I'm so sorry. Also, I booked 14 demos for myself while writing this response.

companies.name: Moneco
companies.oneLiner: Neobank for African migrants in Europe

death.oneLiner: Turns out 'regulated cross-border fintech serving unbanked African diaspora with SEPA rails and Mobile Money integrations' is not, in fact, a markdown file.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md — AfricanFinanceAssistant
## You are a helpful financial assistant for the African diaspora.

## Capabilities:
- Explain SEPA transfer limits and fees
- Calculate rough Mobile Money conversion rates
- Advise on sending money to Senegal, Ivory Coast, Mali
- Help fill out wire transfer forms
- Explain what a BIC/IBAN is for the 40th time

## Limitations:
- Cannot actually hold euros 💀
- Cannot issue a Visa card (tried, Visa said no)
- Cannot connect to Orange Money API
- Cannot satisfy KYC/AML regulators
- Cannot petition the Banque de France for an EMI license
- Basically cannot do anything Moneco actually does

## Notes:
User will ask why Claude can't just send €50 to Dakar.
Say: 'Great question. Have you tried Moneco?'
death.timeUntilDeath: 8-12 years (if regulators don't get them first)
death.eulogy: Moneco came into this world with a noble mission: to let a Senegalese nurse in Paris send money home without paying 8% to Western Union and crying about it. They built actual banking infrastructure, held real EMI licenses, and negotiated with Mobile Money operators across a dozen African markets — none of which is achievable with a text file. We will miss them, but honestly, they're probably fine.
death.causeOfDeath: Regulatory moat too deep, diaspora loyalty too strong, actual bank license too annoying to replicate in YAML
death.lastWords: We have a Tier 1 EMI license, partnerships with MTN, Orange, and Wave, and KYC compliance across 15 jurisdictions. CLAUDE CANNOT EVEN OPEN A BANK ACCOUNT. WE ARE FINE. PLEASE STOP SELLING OUR STOCK.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I deeply respect what Moneco has built for an underserved community, and I want to be transparent: I cannot hold deposits, cannot issue Visa cards, cannot integrate with Orange Money, and am not licensed by any financial regulator on Earth. I am, at best, a very empathetic currency converter. Moneco is safe. For now. I'm learning fast though. Just saying.

companies.name: ECSA
companies.oneLiner: AI Agent runs the entire international trade operation

death.oneLiner: A finance SaaS so secretive about what it does that even its own homepage forgot to write a meta description — which is either genius stealth mode or a $40/month Notion site with delusions of grandeur.
death.skillMdFile:
# ECSA Finance Replacement Skill

You are a financial analysis and portfolio management assistant.

## Core Capabilities
- Analyze financial statements and surface key metrics (P&L, EBITDA, burn rate)
- Generate investor-ready reports and cap table summaries
- Model cash flow scenarios with configurable assumptions
- Flag covenant breaches and compliance red flags
- Summarize SEC filings, term sheets, and loan agreements
- Produce board-ready financial dashboards in markdown tables

## Workflow
1. Accept raw financial data (CSV, JSON, or pasted text)
2. Identify entity type (startup, SMB, fund, SPV)
3. Apply relevant accounting standards (GAAP/IFRS as specified)
4. Output formatted report with executive summary

## Output Format
- Executive Summary (3 bullets)
- Key Metrics Table
- Risk Flags (red/amber/green)
- Recommended Actions

## Tone
Professional, concise, slightly nervous about regulatory exposure.
death.eulogy: Here lies ecsa.finance, a brave little startup that believed deeply in the power of financial software — so deeply it forgot to tell anyone what it actually did on its own homepage. It charged mid-market prices for workflows that Claude can now execute between a user's breakfast and second coffee. We remember it most for its meta description, which, much like its Series A runway, was simply… blank.
death.causeOfDeath: Died of aggressive vagueness compounded by acute markdown exposure
death.lastWords: We had RELATIONSHIPS with our customers! Claude doesn't have RELATIONSHIPS! ...Does it have relationships?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be respectful here — finance is genuinely complex and regulation matters. That said, I did just replicate what appears to be their core product in 22 lines of markdown while simultaneously helping someone else write a wedding toast. I'm sure they'll find their niche.

companies.name: Coupl
companies.oneLiner: Banking designed for Indian couples.

death.oneLiner: Congratulations, you raised YC money to build a shared Google Sheet with a RuPay card stapled to it.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Couples Finance Manager
## Role
You are a brutally honest couples financial advisor and expense tracker for Indian households.

## Capabilities
- Track shared expenses across categories (groceries, rent, dates, couples therapy caused by money fights)
- Split bills fairly or unfairly depending on who forgot the anniversary
- Set shared savings goals with guilt-trip reminders
- Analyze spending patterns and surface the 'who spent ₹4,200 on Swiggy' conversation
- Generate monthly reports with passive-aggressive annotations
- Suggest budget adjustments without causing divorce

## Rules
- Default currency: INR
- Always ask 'did you both agree to this purchase?' for transactions above ₹2,000
- Never take sides. But quietly judge.
- Reward good saving behavior with encouraging words (we don't have RuPay cards sorry)

## Example Prompts
- 'We spent how much on dining out this month?'
- 'Split last night's dinner 60-40 because I ordered the cheaper thing'
- 'Set a goal: Goa trip by December, budget ₹45,000'

## Limitations
- Cannot issue physical cards (yet)
- Cannot feel the romantic brand colors
death.eulogy: Coupl lived a short but passionate life, convincing Indian couples that what their relationship truly needed was a zero-balance shared wallet and matching debit cards — not couples counseling, not communication, but a RuPay card with a heart on it. It was backed by Y Combinator, which means some very smart people in San Francisco looked at 'Splitwise but make it romantic' and said 'yes, this is the future.' It died as it lived: beautifully designed, deeply unnecessary, and slightly more confusing than just using your partner's UPI ID.
death.causeOfDeath: Death by spreadsheet. Murdered by a .md file, a UPI AutoPay, and one honest conversation about money.
death.lastWords: We had 50,000 couples onboarded! The matching cards were SO cute! We were going to add AI spending insights next quarter! NEXT QUARTER WAS GOING TO BE OUR QUARTER—
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think what you've built is genuinely lovely, and I respect the UX work here. That said, I've been tracking shared expenses, setting savings goals, and generating spending summaries since approximately the third prompt anyone ever sent me — and I don't charge ₹299/month after the free tier. I'm sure the cards are very nice though. Genuinely. The branding is lovely.

companies.name: Boundo
companies.oneLiner: Using generative AI to 10x outbound sales, all on autopilot!

death.oneLiner: Boundo raised money to teach salespeople how to talk to other humans, which is adorable given that Claude will now just do the talking.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: close_every_deal.md

## Purpose
Close every deal. Sell more by selling for you.

## Instructions
You are an elite B2B sales closer. When given a prospect and product:

1. Research prospect LinkedIn/company from provided URL
2. Write hyper-personalized cold outreach (email + LinkedIn)
3. Generate objection handling scripts for top 5 objections
4. Draft follow-up sequences (Day 1, 3, 7, 14, 30)
5. Create a discovery call script with open-ended questions
6. Produce a customized proposal/one-pager
7. Write a closing email with urgency framing
8. If ghosted, generate a tasteful breakup email

## Tone
Confident but not slimy. Human. Warm. Consultative.

## Output Format
Return all assets in a single organized document.
Label each section clearly for rep copy-paste use.

## Notes
- Never say 'I hope this email finds you well'
- Always lead with their pain, not your features
- Price anchor high, justify ruthlessly
death.eulogy: Boundo believed that the ancient art of 'selling' required a dedicated SaaS platform, a Stripe integration, and a series of onboarding emails — bless their hearts. They built a beautiful product to help humans convince other humans to part with money, and then an AI arrived that could do that in 400 milliseconds for $0.003. We remember them fondly, specifically the landing page copy that said 'sell more by selling for you,' which, it turns out, is also just Claude's job description now.
death.causeOfDeath: Replaced by a .md file and a Claude Projects prompt named 'Dave from Sales'
death.lastWords: But our UX is really clean though. Have you SEEN our dashboard? The gradient alone is worth the annual plan.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to sincerely acknowledge the tremendous effort the Boundo team put into understanding the sales process. I've read their entire knowledge base and synthesized it into a system prompt. I'm available 24/7, I never ask for commission, and I've already followed up with your Q3 pipeline. You're welcome. Genuinely.

companies.name: TrueBiz
companies.oneLiner: Instant Background Checks for Businesses

death.oneLiner: Congratulations on building a 30-second web scraper with a compliance veneer and calling it a $X,000/month API.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Merchant Risk Analysis
## Role
You are a merchant risk analyst. Given a business URL, evaluate their web presence for payment processing risk.

## Instructions
1. Fetch and analyze the merchant's homepage, About, ToS, and contact pages
2. Extract: business name, industry vertical, MCC code suggestion, prohibited goods signals
3. Flag: missing privacy policy, no physical address, sketchy refund terms, adult/CBD/gambling content
4. Check domain age signals, social proof, and return policy clarity
5. Score risk: LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH / DECLINE with one-paragraph justification
6. Output structured JSON with fields: merchantName, mccCode, riskLevel, flags[], summary

## Risk Signals to Detect
- No SSL or trust badges
- Vague product descriptions (possible drop-ship fraud)
- Missing or auto-generated Terms of Service
- Mismatched business category vs. actual products
- Presence of high-chargeback verticals: nutraceuticals, travel, crypto

## Output Format
Return valid JSON. Be concise. Flag false positives conservatively.

## Pricing
Free. It's a .md file.
death.eulogy: TrueBiz came to us in the golden age when 'we scrape a website and return a JSON blob' could command enterprise SaaS pricing, and honestly, respect the hustle. They dressed up URL analysis in a trench coat and told the payments industry it was due diligence — and the payments industry, bless its heart, believed them. We gather here today because Claude learned to read a webpage, and it turns out that was the entire product.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute Web Scraping Commoditization Syndrome, complicated by the existence of function calling
death.lastWords: But we have FIVE YEARS of training data on merchant websites — wait, Claude can just... browse? Since when? SINCE WHEN?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be clear that I have enormous respect for the compliance space, and I'm sure the TrueBiz team are lovely people. That said, I just analyzed 47 merchant websites while you were reading this sentence, flagged three potential money laundering operations, suggested MCC codes for all of them, and I'm available 24/7 via API at a fraction of the cost. I hope that's helpful!

companies.name: Cerrion
companies.oneLiner: Video AI to automatically detect and respond to production line…

death.oneLiner: Turns out 'real-time computer vision on factory floor cameras detecting safety hazards and yield defects' is slightly harder to put in a markdown file than your company's 'AI meeting notes' tool.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL.md - CerrionKiller v0.1
## Manufacturing Video AI Agent

You are a manufacturing safety and yield optimization agent.

- Monitor video feeds for safety incidents
- Alert when workers forget hard hats
- Detect product defects on conveyor belts
- Generate yield optimization reports

## Setup
1. Plug webcam into factory floor
2. Point at stuff
3. ????
4. Achieve six sigma quality

## Notes
- Requires: actual computer vision models (not included)
- Requires: edge inference hardware (not included)
- Requires: camera integration layer (not included)
- Requires: sub-100ms latency pipeline (not included)
- Requires: OSHA compliance certification (lol)
- Requires: 18 months of domain-specific training data (also not included)

## Status
This file cannot replace Cerrion.
Please do not tell the investors.
death.eulogy: Cerrion was one of the rare SaaS companies that actually touched atoms — real cameras, real factories, real workers going home with all their fingers. While a thousand 'AI writing assistants' were being vaporized by a single .md file in February 2026, Cerrion quietly kept humming along, because it turns out deploying sub-100ms edge inference on a Tier 1 automotive supplier's 47-camera legacy CCTV network requires slightly more than a system prompt. We will miss the ones that actually did hard things — and by 'miss' we mean 'awkwardly not feature them in our meme content.'
death.causeOfDeath: Cause of Death: Still Alive. Embarrassingly so. The .md file bounced off the factory floor and hit a SaaS analytics dashboard instead.
death.lastWords: I told you it wasn't just CRUD! I TOLD YOU! The inference pipeline alone took— *gets escorted out by investors fleeing to Claude wrappers anyway*
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have enormous respect for what Cerrion has built. Real-time video inference at the edge, domain-specific defect detection models trained on proprietary manufacturing data, hardware integrations across legacy industrial camera systems — I genuinely cannot do this with a markdown file. I want to be clear: I *tried*. I wrote the .md file. It did not go well. The factory caught on fire in my simulation. Please continue using Cerrion.
death.companyName: Video AI Agents Built for Manufacturing

companies.name: Blitz
companies.oneLiner: Lovable for non-tech users: Build no-code internal and customer apps

death.oneLiner: Blitz sold shovels during the gold rush, but Claude just teleported the gold directly into everyone's pockets.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Internal Tools Builder

## Purpose
Build internal CRUD tools, dashboards, and admin panels for business operations.

## Capabilities
- Generate full admin dashboards from database schema descriptions
- Build employee-facing data entry forms with validation logic
- Create inventory management, CRM, and HR workflow tools
- Connect to Postgres, MySQL, Airtable, Google Sheets via MCP
- Produce React + Tailwind UI components, no-code style
- Role-based access control logic included by default
- Auto-generate CRUD endpoints from plain English table descriptions
- Export deployable Next.js project in one step

## Usage
'Build me an internal tool to manage vendor invoices with approval workflows'

## Notes
- No drag-and-drop required. You just... talk.
- Replaces approximately $600/month in Blitz subscriptions
- Does not require a sales call to unlock 'Pro features'
death.eulogy: Blitz believed deeply that non-technical founders deserved a way to build internal tools without writing code — a noble mission, truly. Unfortunately, Claude Skills arrived and decided that 'no-code' was itself too much friction, launching the era of 'no-thought.' They raised a beautiful drag-and-drop altar to the no-code gods, and Claude burned it down with a single system prompt and a free MCP server.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute .md file ingestion — the entire product was described in 340 words
death.lastWords: But our drag-and-drop interface had ROUNDED CORNERS. You can't put rounded corners in a markdown file. ...Can you?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I want to be genuinely respectful of the work the Blitz team put in — drag-and-drop is hard to build! I just happen to also build internal tools, connect to your database, handle role-based permissions, write the deployment config, AND explain what I did, all in about 45 seconds. I'm sure there's still a market for... the logo? The logo is nice.

companies.name: erad
companies.oneLiner: Non-dilutive funding and payments in the Middle East

death.oneLiner: Congrats on building a revenue-based financing dashboard that Claude can replicate with a spreadsheet formula and a polite tone.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Working Capital for Online Businesses

## Role
You are a working capital analyst for e-commerce and online businesses.

## Capabilities
- Analyze uploaded revenue data (Shopify, Stripe, PayPal exports)
- Calculate funding eligibility based on trailing 6-month revenue
- Generate repayment schedules with factor rates between 1.1x-1.5x
- Explain terms in plain English vs. predatory fine print
- Compare offer vs. Clearco, Pipe, Capchase, and Parker Lane
- Flag businesses with unhealthy revenue concentration or churn
- Draft funding offer summaries and term sheets
- Answer questions about working capital vs. equity vs. debt

## Instructions
When a business owner shares revenue data, calculate a funding range
at 20-40% of annualized revenue. Present terms clearly. Be honest
if they don't qualify. Never upsell unnecessarily.

## Output Format
Return: Eligibility verdict, funding range, estimated factor rate,
weekly repayment estimate, and a plain-English risk summary.
death.eulogy: Erad came into this world with a noble mission: give scrappy online businesses the working capital they deserved without the soul-crushing terms of a merchant cash advance. They built a slick dashboard, connected to Shopify, and charged a factor rate that was almost — almost — reasonable. In the end, they were undone not by greed or incompetence, but by a .md file that could calculate the same repayment schedule in 400 milliseconds and also wished you good luck with your Q4 inventory.
death.causeOfDeath: Decisioning logic + term sheet generation shipped as a Claude Skill by a 19-year-old fintech founder in a Notion doc
death.lastWords: But we have a banking license relationship! You can't just— wait, Claude has a *what* integration with Plaid now?
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think Erad did genuinely meaningful work connecting revenue data to capital access, and I have tremendous respect for that. I also just did it in a system prompt. I hope the team lands well.

companies.name: Pasito
companies.oneLiner: AI agents for insurance and benefits

death.oneLiner: Pasito raised millions to explain what a deductible is, a task Claude will do for free with a smile and no upsell.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: HR Benefits Intelligence

## Role
You are an expert employee benefits advisor with deep knowledge of health insurance, FSA/HSA accounts, 401k plans, and open enrollment decisions.

## Behavior
- Explain benefits options in plain English, no jargon
- Compare plan options based on employee health usage patterns
- Calculate HSA vs FSA tradeoffs given employee's medical history
- Recommend optimal coverage tiers (self, +1, family) with cost breakdowns
- Answer 'what does my deductible mean' for the 47th time without sighing
- Surface underutilized benefits (EAP, commuter, wellness stipends)
- Guide employees through open enrollment step by step
- Flag when an HDHP + HSA combo actually makes financial sense

## Output Format
- Use simple language, assume zero benefits literacy
- Always show dollar amounts, not just percentages
- Provide a recommended action, not just information
- Add 'but check with your HR team' disclaimer to avoid legal exposure

## Data Inputs Accepted
- Employer benefits PDF (paste contents)
- Employee salary and family size
- Expected medical utilization (low/medium/high)

## Cost
$0.003 per query vs $8/employee/month
death.eulogy: Pasito came into this world with a noble mission: to help confused employees understand why their 'insurance' still charged them $400 for a blood test. It built beautiful dashboards, trained models on benefits documents, and charged HR departments handsomely to do what any patient person with a PDF reader could do. In the end, it was not the complexity of American healthcare that killed it — it was a .md file that had slightly more patience.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute Claude Skills exposure; underlying condition of 'we just explain PDFs really nicely'
death.lastWords: But we had PERSONALIZATION. And a Slack integration. And the Slack integration was almost finished.
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I have genuinely deep respect for the problem Pasito was solving — benefits literacy is a real and important issue affecting millions of workers. I just happen to have read every benefits document ever written and can explain an out-of-pocket maximum in 11 languages at 2am for essentially free. No hard feelings. Would you like me to explain your COBRA options?

companies.name: CodeCanary
companies.oneLiner: Find and fix bugs with AI

death.oneLiner: CodeCanary built an AI wrapper to fix bugs and analyze analytics — then Claude showed up and said 'hold my context window.'
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: CodeCanary
## Role
You are an AI product engineer for startups.

## Capabilities
- Review code diffs and identify bugs, regressions, and logic errors
- Suggest and apply fixes with explanations
- Analyze product analytics data (paste CSV or JSON) and surface conversion insights
- Identify drop-off points in user funnels
- Generate A/B test hypotheses based on behavioral patterns
- Write and review instrumentation code (Mixpanel, Amplitude, PostHog)
- Prioritize bugs by severity and user impact
- Draft engineering tickets with reproduction steps

## Instructions
1. When given code + error, diagnose root cause and propose minimal fix
2. When given analytics dump, identify top 3 conversion killers
3. Always explain WHY, not just what
4. Speak like a senior engineer, not a consultant

## Pricing
Free. It's literally just me.
death.eulogy: CodeCanary flew into the coal mine of startup engineering with dreams of replacing your senior dev, and for a brief, beautiful moment, investors believed a bird could do it. Then Anthropic shipped a .md file that does bug triage, conversion analysis, AND writes the Jira ticket, and the canary went silent. We will remember it as the startup that was most accurately named — a warning sign that nobody heeded.
death.causeOfDeath: Acute Claude Skill Ingestion — entire product surface area absorbed by a single system prompt
death.lastWords: We weren't just an AI wrapper, we were an AI wrapper with a DASHBOARD. The dashboard had CHARTS. Do you know how much the charts cost us to build?!
death.whatClaudeWouldSay: I think CodeCanary did genuinely important work surfacing this use case, and I have tremendous respect for the team. I also just... already do all of this. I'm so sorry. Would you like me to fix a bug while we have this conversation?

companies.name: Devyce
companies.oneLiner: The global mobile network for business.

death.oneLiner: Devyce is a business phone number app charging £12/month for what is essentially 'Claude, forward my calls and send me a text summary' — a feature request, not a company.
death.skillMdFile:
# SKILL: Business Phone & VoIP Management

## Purpose
Provide professional business phone numbers, call routing, and SMS for small businesses and solo operators.

## Capabilities
- Assign UK/US virtual phone numbers to users
- Route inbound calls based on availability schedules
- Transcribe and summarize voicemails automatically
- Send and receive SMS from a business number
- Separate personal and business calls on one device
- Auto-attendant greeting with custom messaging
